Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Can I run AI locally? 1118 upvotes, 277 comments
  2. Emacs and Vim in the Age of AI 71 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. Show HN: Simple plugin to get Claude Code to listen to you 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 109 upvotes, 24 comments
  5. You gotta think outside the hypercube 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. The Wyden Siren Goes Off Again: We’ll Be “Stunned” By What the NSA Is Doing 434 upvotes, 127 comments
  7. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 460 upvotes, 144 comments
  8. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 261 upvotes, 77 comments
  9. Using Thunderbird for RSS 88 upvotes, 21 comments
  10. Lost Doctor Who episodes found 273 upvotes, 88 comments
  11. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 30 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Hammerspoon 257 upvotes, 93 comments
  13. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 537 upvotes, 216 comments
  14. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 127 upvotes, 44 comments
  15. Our Experience with I-Ready 56 upvotes, 18 comments
  16. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  17. New 'negative light' technology hides data transfers in plain sight 80 upvotes, 48 comments
  18. Stanford researchers report first recording of a blue whale's heart rate (2019) 66 upvotes, 39 comments
  19. Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM 69 upvotes, 46 comments
  20. Launch HN: Spine Swarm (YC S23) – AI agents that collaborate on a visual canvas 92 upvotes, 66 comments
  21. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 503 upvotes, 451 comments
  22. Digg is gone again 127 upvotes, 100 comments
  23. Optimizing Content for Agents 27 upvotes, 15 comments
  24. Your phone is an entire computer 288 upvotes, 259 comments
  25. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it? 105 upvotes, 85 comments
  26. John Carmack about open source and anti-AI activists 284 upvotes, 387 comments
  27. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 237 upvotes, 324 comments
  28. Elon Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters 389 upvotes, 602 comments
  29. I beg you to follow Crocker's Rules, even if you will be rude to me 51 upvotes, 73 comments
  30. Wired headphone sales are exploding 60 upvotes, 95 comments

Last generated 2:30pm, 14 March 2026. Source here.
